description: Modern B2B URL shortener with campaign management and analytics. Built with FastAPI (backend), Astro (frontend), and PostgreSQL.

layers: Core Domain (models, auth, database in server/core/)
Frontend (Astro SSG with client-side auth in frontend/)
Schemas (Pydantic models for validation in server/schemas/)
notes: Backend uses SQLAlchemy ORM with PostgreSQL
Tests use in-memory SQLite for isolation

commit_types.chore: Build process or auxiliary tool changes

project_context.development_phase: Phase 3 complete (backend + frontend), Phase 5.1 in progress (testing)
project_context.testing_approach: TDD - all 132 tests must pass before commits

guidelines: Body should be 2-4 sentences max - focus on WHAT changed and WHY
Avoid redundant details that are obvious from the diff
Skip architectural explanations unless critical to understanding
No lists unless absolutely necessary (3 items max)
Commits are not documentation - be concise and actionable

good:
feat(api): add campaign export endpoint

Enables CSV export of campaign URLs with user data. Returns 404 if campaign not found.

fix(auth): handle expired JWT tokens correctly

Previously threw 500 error, now returns 401 with clear message.

test: add auth endpoint coverage

28 new tests for registration, login, and API keys. Suite now at 132 tests.
bad:
feat(api): add comprehensive campaign export functionality

This commit adds a new FastAPI endpoint at /api/campaigns/{id}/export that allows users to export all campaign URLs along with their associated user data in CSV format. The endpoint uses the Campaign model from the Core Domain layer and follows our Clean Architecture pattern. It includes proper error handling for cases where the campaign doesn't exist (404) or the user doesn't have permission (403). The CSV export functionality leverages Python's built-in csv module and returns the data with appropriate headers...

fix(auth): improve JWT token handling

Previously, when a JWT token expired, the system would throw an internal server error (500) instead of properly handling the authentication failure. This was happening because the decode_access_token function in server/core/auth.py wasn't catching the JWTError exception properly. Now we catch the exception and return a proper 401 Unauthorized response with a clear error message telling the user their token has expired. This follows the HTTPBearer authentication pattern we use throughout the application...
